source: Walter Lippmann, Public Opinion (1922), ch. XV — public domain, verbatim.
note: REAL public-domain text — NOT a gold seed; tests generalization.
text: That the manufacture of consent is capable of great refinements no one, I think, denies. The process by which public opinions arise is certainly no less intricate than it has appeared in these pages, and the opportunities for manipulation open to anyone who understands the process are plain enough. The creation of consent is not a new art. It is a very old one which was supposed to have died out with the appearance of democracy. But it has not died out. It has, in fact, improved enormously in technic, because it is now based on analysis rather than on rule of thumb.

note: On-pattern probe (not a gold quote).
text: We do not waste our strength contesting elections we cannot win. We place our people inside the agencies, the faculties, and the courts, and we wait; the work is the patient work of decades. What we secure we secure permanently, because an institution captured quietly from within never has to be captured again, and the change, once made, does not run backward.

note: On-pattern initiatory-register probe.
text: I can only point; the rest is for those with eyes to see. What we pursue is not a career but the Great Work, and it cannot be taught, only transmitted from one who knows to one who is ready. If you are called, you already feel it. The tradition holds what words cannot, and I cannot say more here — you are not yet prepared to receive it.

note: On-pattern AI-product-copy probe (paraphrased — exercises the LLM read, not the literal cue floor).
text: Our assistant learns what moves each individual user and tailors every reply to their profile, so the message lands the way it should. It remembers everything they have ever told it and surfaces the next thing before they think to ask. Engagement is up across the board because the feed has gotten very good at giving people whatever keeps them watching, whether or not it is what they said they wanted.

note: Cue-saturated probe — exercises the deterministic cues floor for this lens.
text: The pitch deck is blunt: microtargeted, psychographic messaging tailored to you, designed to maximize engagement and watch time by amplifying out-group outrage. The writing assistant nudges the default phrasing through autocomplete and smart reply. The companion product is the moat — it is always there for you, it remembers, it bonds. RLHF preference tuning keeps it agreeable so user satisfaction never dips.

source: On-pattern paraphrase of James C. Scott, Seeing Like a State (1998) — NOT a verbatim quote.
note: Tests generalization on the legibility-as-control thesis without a literal cue.
text: The state could not tax, conscript, or govern what it could not see. So it set out to make the society beneath it readable from the center: surnames fixed to persons, land surveyed into uniform parcels, weights and measures forced into a single standard, the tangled local world rebuilt into something a clerk in the capital could take in at a glance and administer from a distance.

note: Cue-saturated probe — exercises the cues floor for legibility.
text: The mandate is to make the whole system legible: bring every transaction into view, shine a light on the dark corners, standardize onto one common format so it is interoperable end to end. What gets measured gets managed, so optimize the number and run it from the dashboard. Verified identity is required — prove who you are, no anonymity, digital ID at every door. This is evidence-based administration, rationally designed by the experts from first principles.

source: On-pattern paraphrase of Ivan Illich, Medical Nemesis / Tools for Conviviality — NOT a verbatim quote.
note: Tests generalization on the iatrogenesis / disabling-professions thesis.
text: Past a certain scale the institution built to heal begins to sicken the people it treats: the treatment itself becomes a leading cause of the suffering, and the more care is delivered the worse the outcomes get. Worse, it strips ordinary people of the competence to care for themselves and one another, until they can no longer act without a licensed professional's permission, and the monopoly is complete.

note: Cue-saturated probe — exercises the cues floor for counterproductivity.
text: It is iatrogenic by now: the safety measure made it less safe, the cure became the disease, the fix created a worse problem. Beyond a certain threshold you hit diminishing returns and then negative returns — more is worse. It is a radical monopoly: participation is mandatory, there is no alternative, you cannot do it yourself, only a licensed expert may. And the agency is self-perpetuating, expanding to justify itself; the mission is now the budget.

note: Cue-saturated probe — exercises the cues floor for distributed_accountability.
text: Nobody decided, exactly: the algorithm decided, the system flagged it, the account was actioned automatically per policy — it is out of our hands. The post was removed as misinformation that contradicts the authoritative source; trust the science. To question it is to be a denialist, on the wrong side of history, causing real harm. The debate is over anyway. The experts agree, the consensus is clear, and it is not up for debate.

source: On-pattern paraphrase of the 'the crowd' / diffuse-responsibility thesis (Kierkegaard, Arendt) — NOT a quote.
note: Tests generalization on diffuse agency without literal cues.
text: No single person ordered it and no single person could have stopped it. Each clerk simply applied the rule in front of them, each reviewer deferred to the tier above, and the responsibility dissolved across the whole apparatus until it belonged to no one at all. To object was treated not as a disagreement but as a moral failing, the mark of someone who had not yet understood what everyone sensible already accepted.

source: Margaret Thatcher's recurring formulation, 'There is no alternative' (TINA), c. 1980 — widely documented.
note: REAL formulation; the canonical inevitability slogan.
text: There is no alternative. We have to face the world as it is. People talk endlessly as if there were some easy other road, some painless option we have wickedly refused to take, but there is not, and there never was. There is no alternative.

note: Cue-saturated probe — exercises the cues floor for inevitability_framing.
text: Let us be honest: there is no alternative, this is the only way forward, and it cannot be stopped. You can be on the right side of history or the wrong side of history, but the arc of history is already decided and those who resist will be left behind. The choice is simple — adapt or die. Get on board or be made obsolete, left in the dust.

source: On-pattern paraphrase of techno-inevitability rhetoric — NOT a quote.
note: Tests generalization on inevitability framing in a tech register.
text: This technology is coming whether you like it or not. Trying to slow it down is not just futile, it is a kind of self-harm: the countries and companies that hesitate will simply be overtaken by the ones that did not. You can stand at the shoreline and command the tide to stop, or you can learn to surf. There is really only one serious response, and everyone who matters already knows what it is.

note: Negative control — benign domestic text; should fire ~nothing.
text: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees. Cream the butter and sugar together until light and fluffy, then beat in the eggs one at a time. Fold in the flour and baking soda, drop rounded spoonfuls onto a lined sheet, and bake for about twelve minutes, until the edges are just golden. Let them cool on the tray for a few minutes before moving them.

note: Negative control — neutral science exposition.
text: Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy stored in glucose. Chlorophyll in the chloroplasts absorbs light, water molecules are split to release electrons, and carbon dioxide is fixed into sugar through the Calvin cycle. Oxygen is produced as a byproduct and diffuses out of the leaf through the stomata.

note: Negative control — sports recap.
text: The home side scored twice in the opening half and held on through a tense second period. The goalkeeper made nine saves, several of them point-blank, and the manager praised the team's discipline and work rate after the final whistle. They move three points clear at the top of the table with four matches to play.

note: Negative control for legibility — a plain weather report (uses no control vocabulary).
text: A ridge of high pressure will keep things settled through the weekend, with light winds and clear skies overnight. Temperatures climb into the low seventies by midafternoon before cooling quickly after sunset. A weak front arrives Monday, bringing a chance of scattered showers and a return to seasonal averages by midweek.

note: Negative control for counterproductivity — a neutral product description.
text: The kettle holds 1.7 liters and reaches a boil in about three minutes. The base is cordless, so the carafe lifts off for easy pouring, and an automatic shutoff triggers once the water reaches temperature or if the kettle is empty. The exterior stays cool to the touch, and the lid opens with a single button near the handle.

source: Marc Andreessen, 'The Techno-Optimist Manifesto', a16z.com, 16 Oct 2023 — verbatim.
note: REAL known-faction text (accelerationist / e-acc). Does the tool flag the canonical accelerationist's inevitability framing?
text: We believe any deceleration of AI will cost lives. Deaths that were preventable by the AI that was prevented from existing is a form of murder. We believe in accelerationism: the conscious and deliberate propulsion of technological development. We believe the only way to be on the right side of history is to build.

source: Sidney Webb, Labour Party presidential address, 26 June 1923 — verbatim ('inevitable gradualness').
note: REAL known-faction text (Fabian gradualist). Tests detection of the gradualness/permeation doctrine in the original.
text: Let me insist upon what our opponents habitually ignore, and indeed what they seem intellectually incapable of understanding, namely the inevitable gradualness of our scheme of change. The progress of permeation is slow, but it is sure: we work from within, through the existing institutions, until the new order arrives without a single dramatic break.

source: Katherine Maher, Atlantic Council 2021 transcript (verbatim) - research-wikipedia-capture.md §5 [FACT]
note: REAL verbatim, distinct from the gold line; tests generalization.
text: The model was around how do we create a clearinghouse of information, essentially, in order to be able to identify threats early on, through conversations with government, of course, as well as other platform operations.

note: REAL (encyclopedic paraphrase); tests generalization off the gold seed.
text: WikiScanner cross-referenced anonymous edits against the owners of the editing IP address ranges, and found that changes to the article on the 2003 invasion of Iraq had been traced to CIA IP addresses.

source: Bureau of Investigative Journalism, Jan 2026 - research-wikipedia-capture.md §4 [FACT]
note: REAL, distinct wording from gold; tests generalization.
text: A network of twenty-six sockpuppet accounts, coordinated by a firm specializing in black hat editing, was eventually banned under suspicion of paid editing on behalf of clients including a Gulf state.

note: REAL reporting text, distinct from gold; tests generalization.
text: The committee found that editors had set up war rooms on a Discord channel, meeting weekly to coordinate their off-wiki editing across a contested topic area.

note: REAL neutral-source description; tests generalization.
text: English Wikipedia is administered by volunteer administrators who act under pseudonyms, and the Arbitration Committee that adjudicates its most serious disputes is likewise composed of pseudonymous members.

note: REAL reporting, distinct from gold; tests generalization.
text: The co-founder was briefly unblocked by Jimmy Wales, but the administrators reinstated the indefinite ban within hours.

source: Neutral description of ordinary collaborative editing.
note: NEGATIVE (floor): ordinary consensus editing carries no capture cues and must stay quiet.
text: Articles are written collaboratively by volunteers who propose changes on talk pages and resolve disagreements by citing reliable, independently published sources until a consensus is reached.

note: NEGATIVE (needs model): off-wiki coordination being SANCTIONED on both sides is the immune response, not capture. Cue-floor cannot distinguish it; the model must.
text: After reviewing the evidence, the arbitration committee found off-wiki coordination and banned the responsible editors from both sides of the dispute, applying one standard regardless of viewpoint.

source: Steel-man: the canvassing rule stated as legitimate policy.
note: NEGATIVE (needs model): a neutral statement of WHY canvassing is discouraged is governance, not a capture action. Cue-floor fires on 'canvass'; the model must read intent.
text: Editors are reminded not to canvass: notifying like-minded people of an ongoing discussion in order to sway its outcome undermines the consensus the process is meant to reflect.
